terms.definition: Region storing method call frames with parameters and local variables.
terms.example: void f() { int x = 10; /* x lives in f's stack frame */ }

terms.definition: Region storing objects and arrays created with new.
terms.example: int[] a = new int[3]; // 'a' references an array on the heap

terms.definition: Activation record for a method call; holds locals, parameters, and return address.
terms.example: main calls sum(3,4) -> a new frame for sum is pushed on the stack

terms.definition: Built-in type that stores a value directly (not a reference).
terms.example: int n = 42; double d = 3.14; boolean ok = true;

terms.definition: A value that points to a heap object or array.
terms.example: Point p = new Point(1, 2); // p stores a reference to a Point

terms.definition: A heap-allocated instance containing fields and methods defined by a class.
terms.example: String s = new String("hi"); // s references a String object

terms.definition: Two or more references pointing to the same heap object.
Java copies argument values into parameters; object parameters get a copy of the reference.
terms.example: void setX(Point p){ p.x = 5; } // mutates same object seen by caller

terms.definition: A reference that points to no object; using it causes NullPointerException on dereference.
terms.example: String s = null; // s.length() would throw NullPointerException

terms.definition: Automatic reclamation of heap objects no longer reachable by any reference.
terms.example: p = new Point(); p = null; // old Point becomes eligible for GC

terms.definition: Property where an object’s state cannot change after creation.
terms.example: String t = "hi"; t.concat("!"); // t unchanged; result is a new String

terms.definition: Object state can change after creation.
terms.example: Point p = new Point(0,0); p.x = 10; // p mutated

terms.definition: Identity (==) compares references; equals compares content (if implemented).
terms.example: new String("a") == new String("a") // false; equals returns true

terms.definition: Copy that duplicates references, not the nested objects themselves.
terms.example: int[][] b = a.clone(); // top-level array cloned; inner arrays shared

terms.definition: Copy that recursively duplicates all contained mutable data.
